title: Core Accounting Theory
subhead: Master the language of business: Accounts, Debits, and Credits.

chunks.title: The Accounting Equation
chunks.bodyHtml:
Everything in accounting is built on one simple equation: Assets = Liabilities + Equity.

If Skarion Manufacturing buys a $10,000 piece of equipment (Asset) using a bank loan (Liability), the equation remains balanced: $10,000 = $10,000 + $0.

chunks.title: Categorize the Accounts
chunks.instruction: Drag each account to its correct classification on the Balance Sheet or Income Statement.

chunks.title: Debits and Credits (DEAD CLIC)
chunks.bodyHtml:
Forget what you know about your personal bank account. In accounting, Debit just means Left, and Credit means Right.

Use the DEAD CLIC acronym to remember what increases an account:

  • Debit increases Expenses, Assets, and Draws (Dividends).
  • Credit increases Liabilities, Income (Revenue), and Capital (Equity).

chunks.title: Your First Journal Entry
chunks.scenarioHtml: Skarion Manufacturing just paid $2,000 cash for this month's rent. Record the journal entry.
